description: A math operator that maps a signal from one range to another

parameterGroups.label: Input Range
parameterGroups.rationale: InputStart and InputEnd define the expected range of the incoming signal. Together they describe the source domain for the mapping.

parameterGroups.label: Output Range
parameterGroups.rationale: OutputStart and OutputEnd define the target range. The input signal is linearly mapped from [InputStart, InputEnd] to [OutputStart, OutputEnd].

gaps.question: How does math.map handle input values outside the [InputStart, InputEnd] range? The hmath.md notes it uses hmath::range for clamping -- is the input clamped to [InputStart, InputEnd] before mapping, or is the output clamped to [OutputStart, OutputEnd] after mapping, or both?
gaps.context: Users need to know whether out-of-range input produces clamped output or extrapolated values, as this affects use in waveshaping and modulation rescaling.

gaps.question: Is the mapping purely linear (i.e., simple proportional rescaling), or does it support non-linear curves (e.g., via skew or additional shaping)?
gaps.context: Determines whether math.map is a simple linear rescaler or if it can produce curved mappings.

gaps.question: Does math.map process per-sample (frame-based) or per-block with SIMD acceleration? What does the process() implementation look like?
gaps.context: Affects CPU cost estimation and understanding of whether this node benefits from block processing.

gaps.question: What happens when InputStart > InputEnd or OutputStart > OutputEnd? Does the node support inverted mappings (e.g., mapping [1,0] to [0,1] for signal inversion)?
gaps.context: Users may attempt inverted ranges for signal flipping; need to document whether this works or produces unexpected results.

notes: math.map is NOT based on the OpNode template used by most math nodes. It has 4 named parameters instead of the single 'Value' parameter. The hmath.md infrastructure doc mentions it uses hmath::range for clamping, suggesting input is clamped before the linear mapping. No cppProperties are set, meaning it is monophonic and not polyphonic -- unlike most math nodes that inherit from polyphonic_base via OpNode.
